# Vertz

> Vertz is a TypeScript stack where types flow from database to browser. Define your schema once with `d.table()` and `d.model()` — get a typed database layer, a typed API with auto-generated OpenAPI, and a compiled UI with fine-grained reactivity. Install everything with `bun add vertz` and import from `vertz/server`, `vertz/db`, `vertz/ui`, `vertz/schema`.

## Critical Patterns (read before generating code)

### Data Fetching (UI)

Vertz automatically generates a typed SDK at `.vertz/generated/client.ts` during development (`vertz dev`) and builds. Create a client wrapper, then pass SDK method calls to `query()` for reactive data. Never pass raw entity name strings.

Client setup (typically in `src/client.ts`):

    import { createClient } from '.vertz/generated/client';
    export const api = createClient({ baseURL: '/api' });

Fetching data in components:

    import { query } from 'vertz/ui';
    import { api } from '../client';

    const tasks = query(api.tasks.list());
    const task = query(api.tasks.get('task-123'));
    const filtered = query(api.tasks.list({ status: 'done', limit: 20 }));

Wrong — never do this:

    query('tasks');
    query('tasks', { limit: 5 });

For custom (non-SDK) endpoints, pass a thunk with a cache key:

    const data = query(() => fetch('/custom/endpoint').then(r => r.json()), {
      key: 'my-custom-data',
    });

List responses use `ListResponse<T>` — access items via `tasks.data?.items`, total via `tasks.data?.total`, pagination via `tasks.data?.hasNextPage`. The shape is `{ items: T[]; total: number; limit: number; nextCursor: string | null; hasNextPage: boolean }`.

Query results are reactive objects with `.data`, `.loading`, `.error`, `.revalidating`, `.idle` properties. Use them directly in JSX — the compiler handles reactivity.

### Forms (UI)

Pass SDK mutation methods to `form()` — never raw strings or URLs:

    import { form } from 'vertz/ui';
    import { api } from '../client';

    const taskForm = form(api.tasks.create, { onSuccess });

Use in JSX: `taskForm.action`, `taskForm.method`, `taskForm.onSubmit`. Wire input names: `<input name={taskForm.fields.title} />`. Per-field errors: `taskForm.title.error`. Per-field values: `taskForm.title.value`.

### Reactivity (UI)

The Vertz compiler transforms plain TypeScript into fine-grained reactive updates:
- `let count = 0` → signal. Assignments like `count++` trigger DOM updates.
- `const doubled = count * 2` → computed. Auto-tracks signal dependencies.
- JSX props with reactive expressions → getters. Parent changes propagate without re-running child components.

Components run once to create the DOM. No re-renders, no hooks, no dependency arrays.

### Styling (UI)

Use `css()` for scoped styles with design tokens and `variants()` for parameterized styles:

    import { css, variants } from 'vertz/ui';

    const styles = css({
      card: ['bg:card', 'rounded:lg', 'p:4'],
    });

    const button = variants({
      base: ['inline-flex', 'rounded:md'],
      variants: {
        intent: { primary: ['bg:primary', 'text:white'], ghost: ['bg:transparent'] },
        size: { sm: ['text:xs', 'px:3'], md: ['text:sm', 'px:4'] },
      },
      defaultVariants: { intent: 'primary', size: 'md' },
    });

### Entities (Server)

Define entities with `entity()` — each generates CRUD endpoints with access control:

    import { entity } from 'vertz/server';

    const tasks = entity('tasks', {
      model: tasksModel,
      access: { list: () => true, get: () => true, create: () => true },
    });

Access rules are required. Operations without access rules don't generate routes (deny-by-default).

### Schema (Database)

Define tables with `d.table()` and models with `d.model()`:

    import { d } from 'vertz/db';

    const tasksTable = d.table('tasks', {
      id: d.uuid().primary({ generate: 'uuid' }),
      title: d.text(),
      completed: d.boolean().default(false),
      createdAt: d.timestamp().default('now').readOnly(),
    });

    const tasksModel = d.model(tasksTable);

Column annotations: `.is('hidden')` excludes from API responses, `.is('sensitive')` excludes from responses, `.readOnly()` excludes from create/update inputs, `.default(value)` makes the field optional on create.

## Getting Started

- [Introduction](/): Overview of the Vertz stack — schema, entities, server, and UI
- [Quickstart](/quickstart): Scaffold a full-stack app with `bunx @vertz/create-vertz-app@latest`
- [Installation](/installation): Add Vertz to an existing project
- [Conventions](/conventions): Code style and patterns for Vertz projects
- [Philosophy](/philosophy): The 8 principles — type-safe, LLM-native, no ceilings

## UI Guides

- [UI Overview](/guides/ui/overview): Compiler-driven reactivity, architecture, and core principles
- [Components](/guides/ui/components): Props, children, conditionals, lists, and component patterns
- [Component Library](/guides/ui/component-library): Pre-built accessible components (Dialog, Select, Tabs, etc.)
- [Reactivity](/guides/ui/reactivity): Signals via `let`, computed via `const`, `watch()` for side effects
- [Styling](/guides/ui/styling): Scoped CSS with `css()`, parameterized variants, design tokens
- [Routing](/guides/ui/routing): Type-safe routing with `defineRoutes()` and `createRouter()`
- [Data Fetching](/guides/ui/data-fetching): `query()` with caching, SSR, optimistic updates, auto-revalidation
- [Auto Field Selection](/guides/ui/auto-field-selection): Compiler-driven column pruning for queries
- [Forms](/guides/ui/forms): `form()` with schema validation, per-field errors, progressive enhancement
- [Auth](/guides/ui/auth): Client-side authentication state and guards
- [Multi-Tenancy](/guides/ui/multi-tenancy): Tenant switching, scoped queries, and isolation
- [SSR](/guides/ui/ssr): Server-side rendering, two-pass data loading, hydration
- [Access Control](/guides/ui/access-control): `can()` checks and `AccessGate` components
- [Icons](/guides/ui/icons): Icon system with tree-shaking

## UI Compiler

- [Compiler](/guides/ui/compiler): How the Vertz compiler transforms TypeScript and JSX

## Schema

- [Schema](/guides/schema): 40+ column types, runtime validation, JSON Schema output

## Errors

- [Errors](/guides/errors): Structured error handling with VertzException subclasses

## Server Guides

- [Server Overview](/guides/server/overview): Entity-driven backend with auto-generated CRUD and access control
- [Entities](/guides/server/entities): CRUD endpoints, access rules, hooks, custom actions
- [Domains](/guides/server/domains): Bounded contexts with automatic route prefixing
- [Entity Exposure](/guides/server/entity-exposure): Control which fields, relations, and filters are exposed
- [Auth](/guides/server/auth): Authentication, RBAC, `defineAccess()`, entitlements
- [Multi-Tenancy](/guides/server/multi-tenancy): Tenant isolation and scoping
- [Code Generation](/guides/server/codegen): Generate typed SDK, entitlement types, and RLS policies
- [OAuth](/guides/server/oauth): OAuth provider integration
- [Services](/guides/server/services): Reusable server-side service layer
- [Environment](/guides/env): Type-safe environment variables with `createEnv()`

## Database Guides

- [DB Overview](/guides/db/overview): Schema-first database layer with typed queries and automatic migrations
- [Schema](/guides/db/schema): Table and model definitions with `d.table()` and `d.model()`
- [Queries](/guides/db/queries): Typed query builder for CRUD operations
- [Migrations](/guides/db/migrations): Schema migrations and diffing
- [Introspection](/guides/db/introspection): Generate schemas from existing databases
- [Seeding](/guides/db/seeding): Database seeding for development

## HTTP Client

- [Fetch](/guides/fetch): Type-safe HTTP client with retries, streaming, and auth strategies

## Deployment

- [Cloudflare Workers](/guides/deploy/cloudflare): Deploy to Cloudflare Workers
- [Static Sites](/guides/deploy/static-sites): Static site generation
- [SSG](/guides/deploy/ssg): Static site generation with data pre-fetching
- [OG Images](/guides/deploy/og-images): Dynamic Open Graph image generation

## Testing

- [Testing](/guides/testing): Test utilities with `createTestApp()` and service mocking
- [Server Testing](/guides/testing-server): Server-side testing patterns

## API Reference

- [Generated SDK](/api-reference/fetch/sdk): Client SDK methods, Result type, QueryDescriptor, ListResponse
- [Reactivity API](/api-reference/ui/reactivity): `signal()`, `computed()`, `effect()`, `watch()`, `batch()`
- [CSS API](/api-reference/ui/css): `css()`, `variants()`, `keyframes()`
- [Router API](/api-reference/ui/router): `createRouter()`, `defineRoutes()`, `useRouter()`, `useParams()`
- [Query API](/api-reference/ui/query): `query()`, `invalidate()`, QueryResult properties
- [Form API](/api-reference/ui/form): `form()`, FormResult, per-field access
- [Context API](/api-reference/ui/context): `createContext()`, `useContext()`, Provider
- [Mount API](/api-reference/ui/mount): `mount()`, `hydrate()`
- [Relative Time](/api-reference/ui/relative-time): Relative time formatting component
- [Foreign](/api-reference/ui/foreign): Third-party component integration
- [List API](/api-reference/ui/list): Virtualized list rendering

## Examples

- [Task Manager](/examples/task-manager): Full-stack example with entities, query, form, and routing
